methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Forge Nano is a Series C climate and energy company that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies—KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—alongside one relevant public comparable and eight additional private peers in the climate tech space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $40M total capital raised and applying a typical 1.7x multiplier for Series C progression, yields $68M. Peer-median analysis across the three direct comparables produces a $1.0B midpoint, with a range spanning $700M to $2.0B. The median valuation across this peer cohort substantially exceeds the stage-progression result, reflecting elevated investor appetite for later-stage climate technology at present.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $58M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $534M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty: stage-progression assumes capital efficiency and returns consistent with Forge Nano's peer stage, while peer-median valuation may embed sector-specific enthusiasm or compositional differences in technology risk and commercialization progress. Resolution of this range hinges on Forge Nano's proprietary technology defensibility, path to revenue, and capital intensity relative to the named peer set.
